pi_items.source: docker-jitsi-meet documentation and setup scripts verify this path is the standard location for custom interface configuration in the /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web volume mount

Confirmed via multiple Jitsi documentation sources and GitHub issues. TOOLBAR_ALWAYS_VISIBLE is a real configuration option in Jitsi Meet that controls whether the toolbar auto-hides after inactivity. While the setting has been moved to config.js as toolbarConfig.alwaysVisible in newer versions, TOOLBAR_ALWAYS_VISIBLE remains supported in interface_config.js overrides for backward compatibility (https://github.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/issues/5455)

Verified via Jitsi documentation and interface_config.js examples. TOOLBAR_ALWAYS_VISIBLE is a boolean setting where true means the toolbar remains permanently visible and false (default) allows auto-hide behavior. JavaScript syntax uses boolean true, not string 'true'

Verified via docker-compose.yml analysis. The docker-compose service is named 'web' at /home/ga/jitsi/docker-compose.yml. When docker-compose creates containers, it uses format {project-name}_{service-name}_{instance}. The project directory is /home/ga/jitsi, so project name is 'jitsi', yielding container name 'jitsi-web-1'

Verified via setup_jitsi.sh (lines 40-42) which creates the directory mkdir -p /home/ga/jitsi and copies docker-compose.yml there. export_result.sh also references /home/ga/jitsi as the location for container operations

Verified via task description and setup_task.sh (lines 41-44). The setup script creates /home/ga/Documents directory and removes old evidence files, and task.json description specifies saving the screenshot to this exact path

privileged_info_summary: This task uses official Jitsi Meet Docker images (jitsi/web:stable-9753, jitsi/prosody:stable-9753, jitsi/jicofo:stable-9753, jitsi/jvb:stable-9753) deployed via docker-compose. The configuration file path /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-interface_config.js is the standard location for custom interface overrides in docker-jitsi-meet, mounted as /config in the web container. The TOOLBAR_ALWAYS_VISIBLE setting is a valid boolean configuration option in Jitsi Meet's interface_config.js that, when set to true, prevents the meeting toolbar from automatically hiding during periods of inactivity. The Docker container name is jitsi-web-1 (derived from project 'jitsi' and service 'web'). The task verifies the configuration by checking file modification time (anti-gaming), container restart time, and collecting evidence via VLM analysis of a screenshot showing the toolbar visibility in an active meeting.
